title: European stock markets open lower due to earnings season and geopolitical tensions
summary: The Ibex 35 and the German DAX fall at the open, as investors assess corporate earnings and uncertainty in the Middle East.

title: Oil surges on fears of a disruption in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Crude oil prices reach $87 per barrel, while the UK sees its borrowing costs rise due to escalating tensions.

title: US inflation falls to 3.5% in June, its biggest drop in six years
summary: The moderation in prices eases pressure on the Federal Reserve and gives financial markets a breather.

title: Asian stock markets rise driven by US inflation data and the AI boom
summary: The Nikkei and KOSPI post strong gains, buoyed by the moderation in US inflation and solid results from ASML.

title: ASML posts 27% higher profit in Q2 and raises its sales forecast for 2026
summary: The Dutch semiconductor company surges on the stock market after beating expectations, thanks to demand for artificial intelligence chips.

title: IBM plummets 25% after cutting its revenue forecasts due to AI spending
summary: The tech giant suffers a historic stock market decline as it loses customers who shift their budget to artificial intelligence, dragging down the sector.

title: Wall Street banks post record profits in the first half of 2026
summary: JP Morgan and other major banks report historic earnings, although their executives warn about the risks of excessive optimism.

title: China's GDP grows less than expected in Q2
summary: The Chinese economy slows down, though Asian markets close higher thanks to the moderation in US inflation.

title: Chip maker CXMT seeks $10 billion in China's largest IPO since 2010
summary: The semiconductor company plans to list in Shanghai to fund its expansion amid growing demand for chips.

title: Stripe and Advent launch a $53 billion bid to acquire PayPal
summary: The payments and private equity firms present a joint proposal to buy the payments platform at $60.50 per share.

title: ECB selects 36 providers to test the digital euro
summary: The European Central Bank advances in the development of the digital currency with a pilot program that will evaluate its technical and operational feasibility.

title: Indra attracts interest from Morgan Stanley after falling 26% on the stock market
summary: Investment bank analysts see a buying opportunity in the Spanish defense and technology company, highlighting its recovery potential.

title: Space sector loses $89 billion in one day despite record orders
summary: Shares of space companies crash on the stock market, although order backlogs reach historic levels, sparking debate on the sector's valuation.

title: Major banks warn of a possible stock market correction due to excessive optimism
summary: Bank of America and other institutions recommend that investors moderate their enthusiasm, noting that markets could be overvalued.

title: Warren Buffett plans to give away all his stake in Berkshire Hathaway by 2034
summary: The legendary investor begins a gradual transition of his business empire, ensuring an orderly transfer of his shares.

title: German prosecutors raid a Deutsche Bank branch over alleged money laundering
summary: Authorities conduct a raid at a bank office as part of an investigation into suspicious financial transactions.

title: Huawei resumes sales of 5G phones abroad for the first time since sanctions
summary: The Chinese company manages to circumvent US restrictions and resumes selling 5G devices in international markets.

title: Sojitz and a US firm to produce gallium in Australia to secure supply
summary: The joint project aims to reduce dependence on China for the production of this critical metal used in semiconductors and electronics.

title: Oil moderates after Trump gives up 20% toll in Hormuz
summary: Crude prices ease after the former president's decision not to impose additional charges on vessels transiting the strait.

title: Spain's inflation stands at 3.2% in June thanks to cheaper fuel
summary: The consumer price index falls slightly, aligning with the Bank of Spain's forecasts and giving consumers some relief.
